name: Build your own React (Pomber)
applicability: You use React 19. Understanding React internals (fiber, reconciliation, hooks) directly improves component design and debugging. This is the canonical 'build React from scratch' tutorial.
home_connection: React 19 in SoNash stack. Informs frontend-developer agent work.

name: A DIY guide to build your own React (Didact)
applicability: Complementary to Pomber's tutorial. Didact focuses on the virtual DOM diffing algorithm. Useful for understanding why React re-renders.

name: How to write your own Virtual DOM
applicability: Conceptual foundation for understanding React's reconciliation. Less hands-on than Pomber/Didact.

name: Build Yourself a Redux
applicability: SoNash uses useState/Context, not Redux. But understanding Redux internals helps appreciate why Context is sufficient for your scale.

name: Tutorial - Write a Shell in C (Brennan)
applicability: Canonical shell implementation tutorial. Relevant if JASON-OS needs custom command dispatch. C implementation, but concepts transfer.

name: Build Your Own Shell using Rust
applicability: Rust shell implementation. If JASON-OS dispatch is built in a compiled language, this is the reference.

name: Write yourself a Git!
applicability: You have heavy git workflows (hooks, pre-commit, pre-push, worktrees). Understanding git internals helps design better hook infrastructure.

name: ugit: Learn Git Internals by Building Git Yourself
applicability: Python implementation of git from scratch. More approachable than the C internals. Good for understanding object model.

name: Writing an OS in Rust (phil-opp)
applicability: JASON-OS is metaphorical, not literal. But OS concepts (scheduling, memory management, process isolation) map to agent orchestration patterns.

name: Build Your Own Database from Scratch (Go)
applicability: SoNash uses Firestore. Conceptual value for understanding query optimization and indexing, not direct applicability.

name: Build your own Redis client and server (Rust/Tokio)
applicability: Tokio tutorial using Redis as vehicle. Async patterns are universal but SoNash is Node/TS, not Rust.

name: Regular Expression Matching Can Be Simple And Fast
applicability: You use regex heavily in pattern compliance scripts. Understanding NFA/DFA theory helps write more efficient patterns. Russ Cox classic.

name: Crafting Interpreters
applicability: Classic PL implementation book. Not directly applicable but widely regarded. If JASON-OS ever needs a DSL, this is the starting point.

name: Command line apps in Rust
applicability: Your statusline is a Go CLI. CLI design patterns (arg parsing, output formatting, error handling) transfer across languages.

name: Build a Regex Engine in Less than 40 Lines of Code
applicability: JavaScript regex engine. Tiny implementation for understanding basics. Less depth than Russ Cox.

name: Building a frontend framework, from scratch
applicability: Builds a framework with templating, state, VDOM from scratch in JS. Deepens understanding of what Next.js/React abstract away.
home_connection: Understanding framework abstractions in SoNash stack.
